skill.name: claude-ally-health
skill.description: Guidance for using Claude as a health information assistant for wellness notes, symptom tracking, and medical question preparation.
skill.summary: Organize health notes and wellness questions with Claude.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two URLs and two weak-cryptography patterns, but the reviewed file contains only descriptive markdown. The URLs are source repository references, and the weak-cryptography matches are false positives in health-assistant text. No prompt injection, executable code, data exfiltration, or hidden network behavior was found.

low_findings.title: Non-executable Repository Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL findings point to GitHub source links in metadata and markdown. They do not initiate network requests or send user data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are visible repository references, not executable code. The skill contains no script or command that would contact the URLs.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm findings occur in plain health-assistant description text. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive computation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain only natural-language skill descriptions. There is no code path where weak cryptography could be used.

content.user_title: Track Health Notes With Claude Ally Health
content.value_statement: Health questions and symptom notes can become hard to organize before appointments. This skill helps Claude structure wellness information, prepare questions, and summarize non-diagnostic health context.
actual_capabilities: Structure health and wellness notes into clear summaries.
Help track symptoms, timing, triggers, and related context.
Draft questions to discuss with a licensed clinician.
Organize medication, habit, and lifestyle notes for review.
Provide general wellness guidance without making diagnoses.
limitations: Does not replace a licensed medical professional.
Cannot diagnose conditions or prescribe treatment.
Depends on user-provided information and may miss context.
Should not be used for emergencies or urgent symptoms.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a Doctor Visit
use_cases.description: Turn recent symptoms, medications, and concerns into a concise appointment brief.

use_cases.title: Track Wellness Patterns
use_cases.description: Organize sleep, nutrition, exercise, mood, and symptom notes into recurring patterns.

use_cases.title: Support Care Discussions
use_cases.description: Summarize observations and questions before discussing care with a family member or clinician.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize My Health Notes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for organizing personal notes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Claude Ally Health to summarize these health notes into symptoms, timing, possible triggers, and questions for my clinician.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Appointment Questions
prompt_templates.scenario: Focused preparation before a medical visit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Claude Ally Health to review my concerns and create a prioritized list of questions for my upcoming appointment.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Symptom Patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Claude Ally Health to compare these daily symptom logs and identify patterns I should discuss with a healthcare professional.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Care Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced summary for caregiver or clinician discussion.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Claude Ally Health to turn these notes into a structured care brief with timeline, current concerns, medications, and follow-up questions.

output_examples.input: Three days of headache notes, sleep logs, caffeine intake, and appointment concerns.
output: A short symptom timeline with dates and severity.
Possible context to mention, such as sleep changes and caffeine intake.
A clinician question list focused on next steps and warning signs.

output_examples.input: A caregiver note about appetite, medication timing, mood, and recent fatigue.
output: A concise care summary grouped by observation type.
Items that need confirmation from the user or clinician.
Suggested discussion points for the next care check-in.

best_practices: Include dates, duration, severity, and relevant context in notes.
Use the output as preparation for professional medical advice.
Remove sensitive details that are not needed for the task.
anti_patterns: Do not ask the skill to diagnose a condition.
Do not use the skill for emergency triage.
Do not rely on generated guidance without clinical review.

faq.question: Can this skill diagnose medical conditions?
faq.answer: No. It can organize information, but diagnosis requires a licensed healthcare professional.

faq.question: Can I use it before a doctor appointment?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize notes and prepare questions to discuss during the visit.

faq.question: Does it access medical records automatically?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill only provides guidance and uses information that the user supplies.

faq.question: Is it safe for emergencies?
faq.answer: No. For urgent symptoms or emergencies, contact local emergency services or a clinician immediately.

faq.question: Can caregivers use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Caregivers can organize observations and questions for family or clinical discussions.

faq.question: Does it make network requests?
faq.answer: No evidence found. The only URLs in the reviewed file are source repository links.
